statement: When nc(B)=1 for all B in [1,256], itl(B) is exactly affine in B with slope delta=(w_prefill(1)+w_decode)/(1+m), and the ITL-binding M can be computed in closed form: M_ITL=clamp(floor(1+(targetITL-itl(1))/delta),1,256), matching a brute scan with zero discrepancy.
regime: nc=1 holds across all B in [1,m_max]
mechanism: Affine structure of itl(B) under nc=1 allows exact closed-form inversion. Confirmed by iter-4 live runs: M_ITL=40 (baseline), 8 (itl-only), 95 (ttft-only), 256 (unbounded), 107 (alpha-low), 6 (alpha-high).
applicability_bounds: nc=1 for all B in [1,m_max]. Breaks when nc jumps (RP-4).

statement: M_ITL is a lower bound on the oracle plateau-onset M*, not M* itself. Realized AvgITL averages over occupancy <=B, so AvgITL<=itl(B); the ITL SLO can be satisfied at batch sizes above M_ITL. The oracle-confirmed occupancy gap M_truth/M_ITL spans 1.59-2.83 on ITL-binding dev scenarios and is NOT a clean constant.
mechanism: M_ITL is the largest B where the ANALYTIC itl(B) <= targetITL; the actual solver uses queue-averaged ITL which is lower, pushing the true onset above M_ITL. Iter-4 live run confirms: occupancy gaps 40→69 (1.73), 8→17 (2.13), 107→170 (1.59), 6→17 (2.83). Also confirmed: ttft-only has M_ITL=95 > M*=92 (M_ITL can OVERSHOOT onset in the ttft-only cell due to TTFT binding constraining the true onset below M_ITL).
applicability_bounds: ITL-binding and crossover cells. For the ttft-only cell, M_ITL > M* (overshoot); use min(M_ITL, M_TPF) as lower bracket.

statement: Saturation throughput S(B)=1000*B/tau(B) is monotonically increasing and concave in B under nc=1, approaching the analytic ceiling S_inf from below. Throughput f(M) is non-decreasing in M and reaches a flat plateau extending to M_MAX on the nc=1 dev set (peak-to-f(256) drop <= 0.0027 on all six). On the 30-scenario benchmark, 23/25 feasible scenarios still have f(256) within 1% of peak, and all 25 within 2.86%. The plateau is present but slightly weaker on the benchmark than on the dev set.
regime: nc=1 holds across [1,256]; dev set confirmed live iter-4
mechanism: Under nc=1, tau(B)=(1+m)*t_iter(B)=(1+m)*(alpha+B*delta) grows slower than linearly, so S(B)=1000*B/tau is concave. The plateau means any M>=M* achieves near-peak throughput. Confirmed by iter-4 naive_max (all 6 scenarios: gap_f<=0.0027 at M=256).
applicability_bounds: nc=1 mandatory. On the benchmark, 2/25 feasible scenarios show f(256) 2-3% below peak (nc>1 jump at high M). Carry nc check into iter-5.

statement: nc=1 is a NECESSARY condition for the ITL closed form to exactly match the brute scan. When nc(B) jumps within [1,256], delta changes at the jump and itl becomes piecewise-affine; a single-slope inversion from B=1 misestimates the crossing by up to one integer step.
regime: When nc varies with B (chunk-size boundary crossings)
mechanism: num_iterations_per_prefill(B) is a step function that increases when the batch-size-to-chunk-size ratio crosses an integer boundary. The dev set has nc=1 throughout [1,256]; the benchmark has cases where nc jumps at high M (e.g., bench-025 nc=67 at M=256).
applicability_bounds: Dev set: nc=1 confirmed. Benchmark: check nc before applying closed form.

statement: M_TPF (largest B with ttft_prefill(B)<=targetTTFT) is an UPPER bound on the TTFT-CONSTRAINT-binding M (where the queue solver's full TTFT first exceeds targetTTFT), NOT an upper bound on the throughput argmax M_truth. The bracket can miss M_truth (ttft-only: M_truth=92 > M_TPF=70) while seed_upper at M_TPF still scores near-zero gap_throughput_rel, because the plateau onset is below M_TPF.
regime: TTFT-binding scenarios where queue wait lifts the true feasible frontier above M_TPF
mechanism: ttft_prefill(B) excludes the M/M/1 queue wait. The actual TTFT includes this wait, so the real TTFT constraint binds at M > M_TPF. Iter-4 h-robustness live confirms: seeding f* at m_max=256 instead of U=70 keeps threshold at true peak, allowing onset search to find M=72 (gap_M=20) rather than collapsing to M~56 (gap_M~36) from undershooting f*.
applicability_bounds: ttft-only cell where M_TPF < M_ITL. In itl-or-crossover cells, M_TPF >= M_ITL and U=M_TPF is reliable as a seed.

statement: The analytic primitives partition params-space into exactly THREE primitive-decidable cells, not four. The 'itl-only' and 'crossover' regimes are indistinguishable from primitives alone — they share an identical primitive signature (itl_binds=1, ttft_pf_binds=0, M_TPF>M_ITL).
mechanism: Cells: (1) unbounded (neither binds), (2) ttft-only (M_TPF < M_ITL), (3) itl-or-crossover (M_ITL <= M_TPF). itl-only and crossover share cell 3.
applicability_bounds: Primitive-level classification only. The oracle can further distinguish sub-cells at runtime.

statement: The relative ORDER of M_TPF and M_ITL — specifically whether M_TPF < M_ITL — is the load-bearing classification signal for the ttft-only regime. The two binding booleans alone are insufficient: the ttft-only scenario binds on both (itl(256)>targetITL AND ttft_prefill(256)>targetTTFT), so a naive 2x2 on booleans misclassifies it as crossover.
regime: ttft-only cell (TTFT constraint more restrictive than ITL)
mechanism: For ttft-only scenario: M_ITL=95 > M_TPF=70. Both itl_binds and ttft_pf_binds are True (itl(256)=30.2>targetITL=60? Actually let me not hallucinate values). The key is M_TPF<M_ITL is the signal. Iter-4 confirms the branch fires correctly, triggering seed=m_max and yielding gap_M=20 (vs ~36 without it).
applicability_bounds: Required in formula_guided.py to select cell-aware seed. Implemented as: ttft_only_cell = (M_TPF < M_ITL).

statement: A downward monotone-predicate binary search bracketed by [min(M_ITL, M_TPF), min(seed, 3*M_ITL)] with MAX_ITERS=6 achieves worst-case calls=8 (including seed probe and harness confirmatory) on all 6 dev scenarios, while achieving worst gap_M=20 and worst gap_f=0.0101 — both strictly within the iter-4 gate (gap_M << both baselines; gap_f < epsilon=0.02).
regime: nc=1 dev set; formula_guided with EPS=0.02, MAX_ITERS=6
mechanism: The occupancy-gap bound 3*M_ITL tightens the upper bracket for ITL-binding scenarios (baseline hi=120 vs U=147, itl-only hi=24 vs m_max=256). For wide-bracket scenarios (alpha-low [107,256]=149 elements, ttft-only [70,256]=186 elements), MAX_ITERS=6 caps binary search early but still converges within gap_M<=20 because the f-plateau threshold crossing aligns within the first few halvings. The EPS/2 threshold margin (0.99*f* vs 0.98 onset definition) ensures gap_f < EPS on all confirmed scenarios.
applicability_bounds: Dev set (nc=1 plateau extends to M_max). For benchmark scenarios where nc jumps or f declines before 256, the plateau assumption may break and MAX_ITERS=6 may not converge cleanly. Verify nc and plateau shape before applying.

statement: A parameter-blind ternary search (naive_ternary) achieves gap_f~0 on all dev scenarios (finds some plateau point) but fails on the concurrency objective: it converges to an interior/high plateau point rather than the onset. On the dev set: worst gap_M=127 (ttft-only: M=219 vs M*=92, alpha-high: M=40 vs M*=17) at 30 calls per scenario. The formula's downward-onset targeting — not search per se — is the component that delivers low gap_M.
regime: nc=1 dev set; monotone-to-plateau f curves
mechanism: Ternary search maximizes f(M). On a long flat plateau, probing m1=lo+(hi-lo)//3 and m2=hi-(hi-lo)//3 yields near-equal f values near the plateau, causing the comparisons to be driven by floating-point noise. The search converges to a point near the middle or high end of the plateau, never to the onset (the SMALLEST plateau point). Without a lower-bound bias from the closed-form formulas, there is no mechanism to push the result toward the onset.
applicability_bounds: Any ternary/golden-section search on a monotone-to-plateau function. The gap_M win of formula_guided is robust to this failure mode because the downward predicate search explicitly targets the smallest M satisfying the threshold.
